channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: HUB SmartCoverage becomes VIU by HUB in Canada
item.description: HUB International has rebranded its Canadian digital insurance platform HUB SmartCoverage to VIU by HUB Canada as part of an effort to unify its digital consumer insurance brand across North America. The platform, which has operated in Canada since 2001, allows consumers to compare quotes from multiple insurers, purchase coverage online, and access licensed brokers throughout the process. “This refresh reflects what we’ve built in Canada over many years and signals clearly where we’re going.

item.title: American Family files trademark for “EV Claims Advantage”
item.description: American Family Insurance has filed a trademark application for “EV Claims Advantage,” signaling a potential expansion of its electric vehicle insurance and claims capabilities. The trademark filing describes the offering as focused on “electric vehicle and electric vehicle charging station insurance,” including claims support and policy administration. Schulz on behalf of American Family Mutual Insurance Company, S.I.

item.title: MetLife Japan raises starting salaries for office-based employees
item.description: MetLife Japan will revise its compensation structure for office-based employees starting June 2026, increasing fixed monthly pay and reducing the emphasis on seasonal bonuses as part of an effort to provide more stable compensation and support long term employee retention. Under the revised structure, the starting salary for university graduates in office-based roles such as agency consulting and actuarial positions will increase from ¥278,000 to ¥400,000 per month, including prepaid severance pay and a fixed overtime allowance equivalent to 20 hours. MetLife said it views employees as the foundation of its business and plans to continue enhancing compensation and workplace benefits to create an environment where employees can work with stability and confidence.

item.title: Pinion Insurance selects Phinsys for finance infrastructure
item.description: Pinion Insurance has selected Phinsys to support the finance infrastructure behind its specialty insurance platform as the business scales its MGA operations. Phinsys will implement its finance automation platform to support Pinion’s finance operations, data integration, reconciliation, and reporting. “As we build a digital specialty insurance platform, having robust and scalable finance infrastructure is essential.

item.title: Tokio Marine receives SX Brand 2026 designation
item.description: Tokio Marine Holdings has been selected as an “SX Brand 2026” by Japan’s Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry and the Tokyo Stock Exchange, becoming the only insurer included in this year’s group of 15 companies. This marks the first time Tokio Marine Holdings has received the SX Brand designation. Tokio Marine said it was recognized for linking the resolution of social issues with corporate value creation, while also achieving profit growth and volatility control through global risk diversification and unified group management.

item.title: AI boom’s supply chain trap
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Shadow Accounting in Insurance Compensation
item.description: Why shadow accounting happens in insurance Insurance compensation is uniquely difficult to manage because it sits at the intersection of money, relationships, compliance, and strategy. But in sales and compensation environments, shadow accounting usually looks less formal, consisting of spreadsheets, offline trackers, manually maintained commission logs, and side calculations used to validate or challenge official payout information. In insurance distribution, shadow accounting often appears when producers, managers, or back-office teams cannot easily see how commissions were calculated, whether adjustments were applied, or how hierarchy and eligibility changes affected payment.

item.title: Buenas Fuentes: Regulador mexicano multa alianza Banorte‑Rappi
item.description: Regulador mexicano multa alianza Banorte‑Rappi El regulador antimonopolio sancionó a Banorte y Rappi por concretar una alianza sin autorización previa . En diciembre de 2025 Banorte se hizo de todas las acciones de Tarjetas del Futuro. La sanción apunta al joint venture celebrado por las empresas para constituir en 2020 Tarjetas del Futuro, empresa que lanzó Rappicard.

item.title: Liberty Mutual Offers Auto Quotes in ChatGPT
item.description: The app lets ChatGPT users obtain auto insurance quotes from Liberty Mutual through a conversational experience designed to be more interactive than a traditional online form. (Image source: Liberty Mutual Insurance.) Liberty Mutual Insurance (Boston) has launched what it describes as a first-of-its-kind carrier-backed conversational AI auto insurance quoting application within ChatGPT. Quotes are available through the app for drivers in seven states, according to Liberty Mutual.

item.title: Verisk Report Shows Rising Roof Loss Severity
item.description: Verisk says everyday wind and hail events, many of which fall below catastrophe thresholds, continued to drive roof claim severity. Verisk says the report draws on property, claims and weather analytics to show how hail volatility and aging roof stock are increasing risk across the U.S. Roofs visibly in moderate to poor condition show approximately 60 percent higher loss costs than roofs in good or excellent condition, according to Verisk Roof Condition Score 2025 baseline data.

item.title: Guidewire’s Mullen Sees Core Systems Anchoring AI
item.description: For John Mullen, President, Guidewire (San Mateo, Calif.), the question is not simply how artificial intelligence will change insurance workflows. That view reflects Mullen’s role at Guidewire, where he oversees field functions including sales, customer success, professional services, solution and industry advisory, and field engineering. You have to be both if you want to navigate and lead in this industry.” Mullen sees that change emerging as many insurers move faster than they have during previous technology cycles.

item.title: Made-in-China EVs Enter Canada Under Deal Carney Struck With Xi
item.description: Chinese-made electric vehicles are beginning to arrive in Canada under a new arrangement that Prime Minister Mark Carney agreed to in January during a visit with China’s President Xi Jinping. The cars will be the first to be imported by Canada under a deal that allows as many as 49,000 Chinese EVs in a 12-month period at a tariff rate of around 6%. The Canada-China deal will be closely watched by U.S.

item.title: Stellantis Recalling 419K US Vehicles for Improper Side Air Bag Deployment
item.description: Chrysler-parent Stellantis is recalling 419,035 vehicles in the U.S. as a software error could delay the side air bags from deploying during a crash, the U.S. This would leave the vehicles non-compliant with U.S.

item.title: Warmer Global Temps to Create Bigger, More Damaging Hailstones: Study
item.description: A warmer world will likely make bigger and more damaging hail, a new study said. Because climate change from the burning of fossil fuels should make more high-energy unstable air, which is conducive to hail forming, global storms pelting roofs, cars and the ground with hail bigger than a large marble will increase between 38% and 47% by the end of the century, depending on how much heat-trapping gas the world spews, a study in Wednesday’s journal Nature said. “And that leads to more thunderstorms with updrafts capable of producing hail.” But with warmer air, there’s less cold as high up for smaller hailstones and they tend to melt more, where bigger ones don’t, Allen said.

item.title: Roof Costs Soar Even as Claims Decline: Verisk
item.description: Roof losses didn’t slow despite a 20 percent decline in overall claims volume in 2025. Roofs that are visibly in moderate to poor condition show approximately 60 percent higher loss costs than roofs in good or excellent condition, according to Verisk’s Roof Condition Score® (RCS) 2025 baseline data. Average residential roof replacement costs reached $17,631 in 2025, with repair costs averaging $4,699.

item.title: Court approves distribution of money recovered from Argento Wealth
item.description: In its 19 May 2026 judgment, the High Court approved pro rata distribution to eligible investors of money recovered by the FCA from Argento Wealth Limited (AWL). You can receive a share of the money recovered if you: We will make payments to eligible investors, as defined in the court judgment, who provide their bank account details on or before 1 August 2026 . If we do not have your bank details by this date, then you will be unable to receive a distribution payment.

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Anthropic Dominates In An Otherwise Slower Week For Megarounds
item.description: This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S. Check out last week’s biggest funding deal roundup here . The 5-year-old generative AI giant secured $65 billion in Series H funding this week, pushing its post-money valuation to a mind-blowing $965 billion.

item.title: Aspen Capital Markets fee income rose 11% in Q1 2026 to $50.6m
item.description: Aspen Capital Markets, the third-party, ILS and alternative reinsurance capital management unit that now falls under Sompo after its acquisition of the re/insurer, reported another increase in fee income generated from its work with third-party capital partners in the first-quarter of 2026. For Q1 2026, Aspen Capital Markets fee income is reported to have risen to $50.6 million, an 11% increase from the prior year’s $45.6 million. Recall that, Aspen Capital Markets grew its assets under management AUM by 23% to a new high of almost $2.73 billion over full-year 2025 .

item.title: Palomar extends earthquake reinsurance tower to $3.92bn at June renewal, cites ILS capacity
item.description: Palomar Insurance Holdings, the speciality US insurer that offers largely catastrophe exposed property products, has lifted the top of its reinsurance tower to provide as much as $3.92 billion of earthquake reinsurance protection at the renewals, with multi-year catastrophe bonds set to provide $1.28 billion of coverage for 2026. For its reinsurance renewal for the 2026-2027 year, Palomar has added $421 million in incremental limit, to lift its tower to the new $3.92 billion level, up from $3.53 billion a year ago. Much like in previous years, protecting against major earthquake events in its home state of California, as well as other quake-exposed regions of the US, remains a core focus of Palomar’s reinsurance program.

item.title: Aspen’s underwriting income hits $79.1m in Q1’26 as CoR improves
item.description: Aspen Insurance Holdings has reported a sharp improvement in underwriting performance for Q1 2026, with underwriting income almost tripling year-on-year to $79.1 million as the combined ratio improved to 89.1% from 96.1% a year earlier. Despite the stronger underwriting and operating performance, Aspen reported a net loss after tax of $55.6 million for the opening quarter of 2026, compared with net income of $36.8 million in Q1 2025. Aspen’s operating income in Q1 2026 rose to $85 million from $50.4 million in the same period of 2025, while net investment income edged higher to $77.5 million from $75.9 million.

item.title: Florida property cat reinsurance market ‘as strong as ever’, says Aon’s Chris Dittman
item.description: Chris Dittman, Executive Managing Director and Florida Practice Leader at broking group Aon, views the Florida property catastrophe reinsurance market as the healthiest and most stable it has ever been, pointing to stronger balance sheets, solid underwriting performance, increased capital levels, and continued stabilisation following recent reforms. During AM Best’s Analytical Briefing on the state of the Florida property market, Dittman stated that the current environment in Florida is very healthy, both from an insurance and reinsurance perspective. “The balance sheets are solidified, driven by meaningful underwriting margins, reduced loss costs, and strong rate adequacy.” Dittman continued, “Aon tracks 50 plus Florida focused personalised property carriers, and within that group, we’ve seen the statutory surplus levels grow from an aggregate of roughly $4.5 billion five years ago to over $9.2 billion this past year end.

item.title: Corpus Christi Focuses on Delaying Looming Water Crisis
item.description: Corpus Christi is bracing for demand to exceed water supplies by next summer, leaving far too little time to dodge a crisis by building new infrastructure, said Kenneth Dees, a water resources engineer based in Fort Worth. Five straight years of record heat, sporadic rainfall and divided leadership has Corpus Christi in danger of becoming the first U.S. At this point, he said, “the only thing that you could do is stop using water.” City leaders are left searching for ways to delay, not avoid, the looming emergency through conservation efforts, such as mandatory water restrictions and higher fees for exceeding limits.

item.title: Kentucky Food-Color Plant Was ‘Catastrophe Waiting to Happen,’ CSB Says
item.description: A Kentucky food-coloring manufacturer made a number of missteps and was not equipped to prevent the 2024 runaway chemical reaction that killed two workers and caused millions of dollars in damage to the plant and surrounding neighborhoods. “This tragic incident was a catastrophe waiting to happen,” Chemical Safety Board (CSB) Chairman Steve Owens said in a statement. “The reactor’s pressure relief system was not designed to release pressure from a reaction like this, and Givaudan did not recognize the potential for a runaway reaction to happen.” The CSB, an independent, nonregulatory federal agency that investigates incidents, explained that a reactor, used to produce caramel coloring for food products, had been relocated to the site from an older facility.

item.title: Illinois Passes Legislation to Give Insurance Department Oversight of Rate Changes
item.description: Illinois leaders for years have pushed lawmakers to pass legislation that would give the insurance department more power to reign in rate increases. HB 4273 and SB 714 give the Illinois Department of Insurance the ability to review and overturn rate increases that are deemed “excessive, inadequate or unfairly discriminatory.” Brian Christenberry, regional vice president for NAMIC, said the bills add a tighter regulatory framework without addressing the underlying drivers of premium increases. Illinois lawmakers this week passed a pair of bills that gives the insurance department the authority to review homeowners and auto rate filings to determine whether they are excessive.

item.title: Luminor launches E&S property MGA
item.description: Luminor launched as a new managing general agent focused on the U.S. excess and surplus commercial property market, targeting catastrophe-exposed and hard-to-place risks through a wholesale-only distribution model. According to the company, the platform combines experienced E&S property underwriting leadership with proprietary data, analytics, and AI-driven underwriting infrastructure designed to support submission intake, pricing, risk selection, and portfolio management.

item.title: Floodbase launches parametric flood quote API
item.description: Floodbase has launched a Quote API designed to automate and scale parametric flood insurance quoting workflows for insurers and MGAs. Floodbase said the API also enables carriers and MGAs to design geographically distributed parametric flood programs covering thousands of locations simultaneously, supporting products ranging from embedded insurance add-ons to reinsurance solutions. Founded in 2017, Floodbase provides satellite and AI-driven flood monitoring technology used in parametric insurance products.

item.title: Guardian names Nancy DeRusso Head of Client Solutions
item.description: Guardian announced the appointment of Nancy DeRusso as Head of Client Solutions, a role focused on advancing the company’s protection-first approach to wealth management. DeRusso will oversee Guardian’s protection and wealth strategy, with responsibilities spanning planning, investment capabilities, and advisor support. She joins Guardian from Goldman Sachs Ayco, where she spent more than 20 years in leadership roles, most recently serving as Head of Financial Planning, Financial Wellness, and SurvivorSupport.

item.title: Jefferies Sued by Fund Investors Alleging Water Firm Fraud
item.description: has been hit with an investor class action lawsuit over bond purchases made by its 352 Capital fund in a water vending machine firm that federal prosecutors have called a scam. According to the complaint filed in New York state court, Leucadia Asset Management’s 352 Capital poured millions of dollars into bonds issued by Water Station Management, which the government and Jefferies itself have alleged was a fraud. In the latest lawsuit, the fund’s investors allege that Leucadia approved these bond purchases despite knowing the underlying collateral did not exist, and despite awareness that Chirico was personally invested in the water machine business.

item.title: With a Super El Niño on the Horizon, Insurers Have Narrow a Window to Get Communications Right
item.description: With Super El Niño impacts expected to begin as early as the end of summer , the window for meaningful policyholder outreach is already open. Climate scientists are increasingly confident that a Super El Niño will emerge later this year—and that it will rank amongst the most powerful on record—impacting global temperature and rainfall patterns. One of the most important things insurers can do right now is make sure policyholders know exactly what their coverage includes—and excludes—before they need to use it.

item.title: Seadrif, UN launch anticipatory drought insurance pilot in Laos
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Navigating The DPI Crunch And Startup Funding
item.description: AI’s share of venture funding climbed to 80% this quarter, up from 55% a year ago. The exit window for American founders is narrower than the headline funding numbers suggest. Crunchbase reported that global venture deployment hit roughly $300 billion in Q1 2026, with $188 billion of that, about 65%, concentrated in four companies: OpenAI , Anthropic , xAI and Anduril Industries .

item.title: They Saw Women Shut Out Of VC, So A PayPal Veteran And Former Navy Officer Built An Alternative
item.description: That’s why former PayPal executive Molly Huyck teamed up with Amie Konwinski , a U.S. Navy veteran and marketing executive, to found Aequitas Invest , an SEC -registered, funding portal. Crunchbase News recently spoke with Huyck and Konwinski to hear more about what led them to start AQi, why they think women don’t need to give up board seats early on, and how they want to help female entrepreneurs raise and hold on to more equity.

item.title: CEA targets upsized $400m of earthquake reinsurance from Sutter Re 2026-1 cat bond
item.description: The California Earthquake Authority (CEA) is looking to upsize its latest catastrophe bond sponsorship, with the target raised one-third to $400 million of fully-collateralized earthquake reinsurance protection from this Sutter Re Ltd. For this Sutter Re Series 2026-1 issuance, the CEA was initially targeting $300 million of fully-collateralized earthquake reinsurance from the capital markets. Last year, the CEA successfully sponsored three series of catastrophe bonds, securing the company in total $1.57 billion of earthquake reinsurance protection from the cat bond market.

item.title: Our track record, collaborative approach & robust investment due diligence sets Elementum apart: Paul Barker
item.description: Paul Barker, Partner and Chief Investment Officer, Private ILS at alternative investment manager Elementum Advisors, LLC, explores how the specialist insurance-linked securities (ILS) manager positions itself within a competitive landscape, and how its approach has adapted amid an increasingly sophisticated investor base. But for us, what really sets Elementum apart is our collaborative approach with both cedants and our clients (investors), our ability to create customized solutions, and the flexibility within our approach to respond to the market environment. “In terms of what sets us apart, our long-standing track record across multiple market cycles is an obvious differentiator.

item.title: Despite softening, reinsurance pricing at 2021/2022 levels “widely viewed as adequate” – KBW
item.description: In general, analysts from KBW heard during a recent Bermuda trip that reinsurance pricing is down about 20% at the mid-year renewals (the report is headlined “20% is the new 15%”), but even after the softening taking rates back to levels seen in 2021 to 2022, this is still “widely viewed as adequate”, the analysis explains. Summing up on pricing that, “We heard several comparisons of this year’s pricing to 2021/2022 levels, which is still widely viewed as adequate and a far cry from the low point of 2017.” Terms and conditions are seen as “mostly holding”, albeit with some concessions that improve coverage for reinsurance buyers. KBW’s analysts said, “Most reinsurers appear far more willing to accept lower pricing than to yield changes on the much improved T&C changes that were achieved at the 1/1/2023 onset of the recent hardening cycle.” But, the analysts cite a “gamut of minor T&C changes”, such: as minimum premium true-ups to exposure now being allowed to float down and up; Florida reinsurance premiums now being paid quarterly instead of upfront; broadening of retrocession covers to named natural perils, from named specific perils (this is one to watch if events happen); aggregates, cascading covers, and top and drops now being more widely available; plus some modest extension of hours clauses and radius definitions (again, ones to watch when events occur).

item.title: T&Cs hold firm, catastrophe reinsurance rates down closer to 20% at mid-year renewals: KBW
item.description: KBW, the investment bank and financial services research firm specialising in the insurance sector, has indicated a more challenging mid-year catastrophe reinsurance renewal environment for sellers, with pricing reductions nearer 20% than 15%, particularly for Florida-focused business, while terms and conditions (T&Cs) are seen as largely holding firm. On pricing, KBW estimated that headline risk-adjusted reductions for mid-year property catastrophe renewals are averaging around 20%. While property and property cat reinsurance rates continue to soften, KBW noted that the more significant development across the market is that the stricter terms and conditions introduced during the 2023 hardening cycle have largely remained intact, despite a series of smaller concessions beginning to emerge.

item.title: Vishal Desai appointed as International Chief Risk Officer at MS&AD Insurance Group
item.description: MS&AD Insurance Group, a major Japanese non-life insurance group with a substantial international presence, has appointed Vishal Desai to a senior global role as International Chief Risk Officer. In this capacity, Desai will join the International Executive Committee at MS&AD Insurance Group, working alongside experienced colleagues from across the organisation’s global network. He will collaborate with entity leadership teams to support the continued development of MS&AD Insurance Group as an international insurance and financial services group, with a focus on strengthening risk oversight, compliance and legal governance across the International portfolio.

item.title: Palomar’s earthquake reinsurance limit up to $3.92bn after June renewal
item.description: Palomar Holdings, Inc., a US-based specialty insurance company focused on property and casualty coverage, has successfully completed its June 1st reinsurance placement, securing approximately $421 million of incremental limit to support the growth of its earthquake franchise. Mac Armstrong, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Palomar, said, “We are very pleased with the outcome of our June 1 reinsurance placement and remain grateful for the support of our broad and diversified reinsurance panel. The company’s reinsurance coverage now extends to $3.92 billion for earthquake events and $135 million for continental US hurricane events.

item.title: Viewpoint: Somali Piracy Is Back – Fueled by Political Turmoil, Aid Cuts and Iran War
item.description: In our 2014 research on the local dynamics of Somali piracy, we found that peaks in local pirate activity coincided with periods of political turmoil and military contest. Together, these incidents have sparked concern over whether we’re seeing a resurgence of Somali piracy. Since then, Somali piracy has been kept in check by a combination of private security guards, naval patrols and land-based development initiatives.

item.title: Number of Deaths Rises in Washington Paper Mill Explosion
item.description: At least eight people are confirmed dead, and authorities believe there are three more bodies they have yet to reach at the site of the paper mill explosion in Longview, Washington. Two people had previously been pronounced dead, bringing the total number of deaths to 11. Related: Fatalities Reported in Washington State Packaging Facility Explosion Responders couldn’t say when they expected to recover the final three bodies.

item.title: Ships Attacked in Strait of Hormuz This Week, Chevron CEO Says
item.description: Several vessels transiting through the Strait of Hormuz have been attacked in recent days, underscoring the “very real” risks that remain for shipowners in the Persian Gulf whether or not a peace accord is signed, Chevron Corp. “They’re maybe not every day, but there have been multiple incidents that have occurred.” Chevron would not consider paying a toll to move ships through the Strait of Hormuz, Wirth said. “We see risks very real still in that environment.” Asked to clarify what hadn’t yet been reported, Wirth said several ships have been attacked.

item.title: Higginbotham expands midwest presence with McGohan Brabender
item.description: The partnership expands Higginbotham’s presence in the Midwest and adds employee benefits capabilities across compliance, pharmacy, and self funded insurance. Higginbotham announced an agreement to join forces with McGohan Brabender , an employee benefits company based in Dayton, Ohio with six offices across Ohio, Indiana, and Kentucky. McGohan Brabender clients will also gain access to Higginbotham’s property and casualty, personal lines, and wealth management offerings.

item.title: Howden expands into aviation insurance
item.description: Howden launched a US Aviation Practice focused on risk management services and insurance placement for aviation and aerospace clients across the country. Meanwhile, the insurance industry continues to underinvest in and underservice a sector that is incredibly complex, dynamic, and exposed.” – Julio Jimenez, Aviation Practice Leader, Howden US Specialty . Mike Parrish, CEO of Howden Americas, said the new practice gives aviation clients access to global capabilities and specialized expertise.

item.title: Lemonade centralizes internal knowledge with Guru
item.description: Lemonade is using Guru as an internal knowledge management platform to help employees across customer service, claims, and underwriting access and maintain insurance-related information. Lemonade’s use of Guru reflects a broader push across insurance to centralize internal knowledge and workflows using AI and automation tools. According to Guru, Lemonade built a workflow using Guru, Slack, Zapier, Google Sheets, Zendesk, and 360Learning to centralize knowledge requests and updates across teams.

item.title: [Exclusivo] Bankaool de México a poco de cerrar adquisición de banco en Brasil
item.description: La tradicional ruta de expansión de Brasil a México ahora tiene viaje de regreso, debido a que el grupo costarricense Omni, accionista mayoritario de Bankaool de México, está a poco de concretar la compra de un banco en Brasil. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: Otro banco internacional toca la puerta de Perú, revela el regulador
item.description: Esta semana se conoció oficialmente que representantes de un banco global se reunirán dentro de poco con el regulador de sistema financiero de Perú para conocer el proceso de aplicación a una licencia bancaria en el país andino. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: Mapfre lidera al seguro español en el ranking de marcas más valiosas
item.description: En conjunto, el ranking refleja una evolución desigual dentro del sector asegurador español, con Mapfre ganando posiciones mientras otras compañías registran movimientos más limitados. Mapfre refuerza su liderazgo como principal marca aseguradora en España y mejora su posición en el ranking nacional de Brand Finance . En 2025, las marcas más valiosas de España alcanzaron un valor conjunto de 132.200 millones de euros, impulsadas principalmente por el buen desempeño de la banca, las aerolíneas y el sector cervecero, que lideran el crecimiento del valor de marca en el país.

item.title: Las grandes aseguradoras disparan su valor de marca un 14% y marcan su mejor resultado en cinco años
item.description: El sector asegurador mundial ha alcanzado su mejor desempeño en valor de marca en los últimos cinco años, según ha dado a conocer Bran Finance . En la cúspide del ranking se mantiene Ping An Insurance , que supera los 39.900 millones de dólares de valor de marca tras avanzar un 19% y encadena una década como líder mundial. El ranking refleja además un refuerzo del peso de Estados Unidos y China en el sector , que representan conjuntamente casi la mitad del valor total del ranking y marcan el ritmo de crecimiento, apoyados en la escala de sus mercados y en la mejora de la eficiencia operativa.

item.title: Assicuratrice Milanese anuncia su entrada en Caución y pone al frente a Julent Augusto Cerezo
item.description: Como dio a conocer ayer por su perfil de LinkedIn, Assicuratrice Milanese España sigue reforzando su presencia en el mercado español e inicia su desarrollo en el ramo de Caución. Como primer paso, h a incorporado a Julent Augusto Cerezo Cendales como responsable de Suscripción del ramo en España, reportando directamente a Marco Zamparo , director del Ramo de Cauciones, ambos en la foto. En este sentido, adelanta que pondrá el foco en: Caución Técnica frente a la Administración Pública; Puntos de Conexión; y Garantías de Afianzamiento de Cantidades Entregadas a Cuenta.

item.title: Trump Files Fresh $10 Billion Suit Over WSJ’s Epstein Story
item.description: over an article about his alleged close ties to Jeffrey Epstein, after an earlier version of the suit was tossed out by a judge. The revised suit was filed late Wednesday in federal court in Florida, meeting a deadline set by a judge who dismissed the original complaint for lacking sufficient claims that the story was filed with “actual malice” toward Trump, a high bar set for libel suits by public figures. Trump sued over a July 2025 story that said he’d sent a “bawdy” birthday note to the disgraced financier in 2003, typed within a sketched outline of a naked woman and signed with the president’s signature in the pubic area.
